skill.name: stitch-design-taste
skill.description: Creates semantic DESIGN.md guidance for Google Stitch projects, with opinionated rules for typography, color, layout, motion, responsiveness, and visual anti-patterns.
skill.summary: Generate premium design-system guidance for Google Stitch screens.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high-risk patterns, but contextual review found documentation-only Markdown, not executable code. The command, cryptography, and reconnaissance matches are false positives from inline code formatting, hex colors, and design-system language. The only residual concern is low-risk external web references used for Google Stitch access and placeholder images.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Code Marked as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported external command matches occur inside Markdown prose and inline code examples for design values, CSS properties, and file names. I found no shell script, Ruby backtick execution, subprocess call, or instruction to run arbitrary commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown design guidance with backtick formatting, not executable code. The skill contains only SKILL.md and DESIGN.md, so there is no runtime command path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Design Values Marked as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings appear to match substrings in a skill name, Markdown headings, and color or typography values. I found no MD5, SHA-1, DES, cipher configuration, hashing API, or authentication logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is visual design documentation, especially font names, headings, and hex colors. No cryptographic operation or security-sensitive data flow is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Design-System Text Marked as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance matches are ordinary design-system and viewport-testing language. They do not collect hostnames, environment details, file listings, user data, or system metadata.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched text describes layout and responsive design requirements. There is no behavior that probes the local system or gathers deployment information.

low_findings.title: Low Risk: External Web References in Documentation
low_findings.description: The skill references Google Stitch access and suggests placeholder images from picsum.photos. These are user-facing documentation references, not hidden network calls, but users should understand that generated designs may include external assets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible in documentation and there is no code that automatically sends requests. The residual risk is limited to external dependency and privacy expectations for generated assets.

content.value_statement: Generic AI screens often look repetitive, fragile, and hard to refine. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a precise DESIGN.md standard for premium Stitch interfaces.
actual_capabilities: Defines a semantic DESIGN.md structure for Google Stitch screen generation.
Documents color roles with exact hex values and banned palette patterns.
Specifies typography rules, font choices, spacing, and responsive behavior.
Lists component guidance for buttons, cards, inputs, loaders, and error states.
Captures motion intent for later implementation by a coding agent.
Provides anti-pattern rules that reduce generic AI-generated UI output.
limitations: It does not generate live application code or run design validation tests.
It depends on the model applying the written design rules consistently.
It is strongly opinionated and may not fit conservative enterprise brand systems.
External image suggestions may need replacement for production privacy or licensing needs.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Stitch Project Brief
use_cases.description: Create a clear DESIGN.md before asking Stitch to generate screens for a new product concept.

use_cases.title: Standardize AI UI Output
use_cases.description: Give coding agents a shared visual standard before converting static Stitch screens into application code.

use_cases.title: Improve Client Concept Quality
use_cases.description: Use the anti-pattern list and design rules to create more polished first-round interface concepts.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Design Standard
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new Stitch project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use stitch-design-taste to create a DESIGN.md for a simple product dashboard. Keep density balanced, use one accent color, and include responsive rules.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt the Taste to a Brand
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate customization for an existing brand direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a DESIGN.md for a finance app with a calm, premium mood. Keep the palette restrained, avoid neon colors, and define typography, cards, inputs, and motion intent.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Stitch Screen Direction
prompt_templates.scenario: Review before regenerating or refining screens.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare this screen brief against stitch-design-taste. Identify generic patterns, weak typography choices, color issues, layout risks, and missing responsive rules.

prompt_templates.title: Produce a Production Handoff Standard
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced handoff from static Stitch output to implementation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a complete DESIGN.md for a multi-page SaaS workflow. Include semantic color roles, component behavior, motion implementation notes, viewport rules, and explicit banned patterns for the coding phase.

output_examples.input: A founder needs a design standard for a new analytics dashboard in Google Stitch.
output: A DESIGN.md with a restrained visual atmosphere and balanced density.
A single-accent palette with exact color roles and banned color patterns.
Dashboard-specific typography, component, grid, and responsive guidance.

output_examples.input: A designer wants to make a Stitch landing page feel less generic.
output: A revised hero direction with asymmetric layout guidance and stronger image use.
A list of banned AI design patterns such as neon glows and filler scroll prompts.
Specific rules for typography scale, spacing, CTA restraint, and mobile behavior.

output_examples.input: An engineer is converting Stitch screens into a live frontend.
output: Motion intent documented with spring behavior and hardware-friendly animation guidance.
Component notes for buttons, forms, loaders, empty states, and errors.
Implementation constraints for responsive layouts and performance-sensitive effects.

best_practices: Set the creativity, density, variance, and motion levels before generating the design standard.
Replace placeholder image services with approved brand assets before production use.
Use the anti-pattern list as a review checklist after Stitch creates screens.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a substitute for accessibility, usability, or brand compliance review.
Do not keep external placeholder images in production handoffs without approval.
Do not apply the opinionated visual style unchanged to every industry or audience.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates structured DESIGN.md guidance for Google Stitch and related AI design workflows.

faq.question: Does it generate application code?
faq.answer: No. It creates design-system instructions that a model or developer can use before implementation.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Is the design style neutral?
faq.answer: No. It is intentionally opinionated and favors premium, asymmetric, anti-generic interfaces.

faq.question: Are there security concerns?
faq.answer: The reviewed files are documentation only. External web references are visible and should be reviewed before production use.

faq.question: When should I avoid this skill?
faq.answer: Avoid it when a project must strictly follow an existing brand system or a conservative UI standard.
